_description: System for generating ad concepts from brand vault + audience + hook frameworks. This is the 'I need 10 ads' engine.

how_to_use.step_1: Load brand.json and product.json for the target brand
how_to_use.step_4: Generate concepts by matching product ad_angles with hook frameworks and audience pain_points
how_to_use.step_5: Output 10-15 concepts with enough detail to evaluate before scripting

concept_output_format.hook_line: [the text overlay / opening line that stops the scroll]
concept_output_format.story_summary: [2-3 sentences describing what happens in the ad]
concept_output_format.key_claim: [the ONE product claim this ad centers on]
concept_output_format.character_notes: [which character archetype fits best, or existing locked character]
concept_output_format.confidence: [HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW based on how well angle + hook + audience align]

concept_generation_rules: Every concept must center on ONE specific product claim or angle. Not multiple
Match hook type to audience pain point. Skeptic audiences get skeptic-to-believer hooks, not morning routine hooks
Generate at least 2 concepts per hook type to test different angles within the same framework
Include at least 3 HIGH confidence concepts (strong angle-hook-audience alignment)
Include 2-3 experimental/unexpected concepts (MEDIUM confidence, could outperform)
Never generate a concept that requires claims the brand can't support
Consider what's working for competitors (from brand.json competitive_landscape) and do it better or different
If performance data exists from previous campaigns, weight toward proven hook types and angles

concept_matrix_example.description: Example: LYFEfuel x Skeptical Sarah x 10 concepts

approval_workflow.step_1: Present all 10-15 concepts with confidence ratings
approval_workflow.step_3: Approved concepts move to script-structures.json for scripting
approval_workflow.step_4: Rejected concepts archived with rejection reason (feeds back into future generation)
